steps.title: Identify which terms can be combined
steps.content: Look at $6x + 5x + 4y$. The terms $6x$ and $5x$ both contain the variable $x$ with the same power (the first power), making them like terms. The term $4y$ contains a different variable and therefore cannot be combined with the $x$ terms.

steps.title: Combine the like terms
steps.content:
Add the coefficients of the like terms:
$$6x + 5x = (6 + 5)x = 11x$$
The complete simplified expression is $11x + 4y$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
